what_it_does: Introduces the Design DNA deploy kit, lists its machine-loadable files, explains the generation workflow, states the main signal-economy rule, and records open confirmation items.
observations: Frames the directory as paste-ready extracts for a content-generation system.
Names four kit files: tokens.yaml, prompt-image.txt, prompt-layout.txt, and prompt-copy.txt.
Defines a five-step usage flow from dial selection through invariant checking.
Emphasizes signal economy, quiet large elements, rare gold usage, and negative/fail blocks as controls against excessive color.
Lists unresolved items for typeface confirmation and high-warmth imagery quality.
standouts: Signal economy is presented as the central rule.
The workflow treats tokens.yaml as the style source of truth.
Invariant checks are described as decisive for whether output is on-DNA.
conventions: Uses a short README structure with sections for files, usage, rule, and open items.
Uses bullet lists and a numbered process for operational readability.
Keeps the language directive and implementation-facing rather than descriptive-only.
Uses explicit file names and dial names as configuration handles.
concerns: Two open items remain unresolved in the file.
The README references section 6 quality gates in another document without restating them here.
notes: A compact operational index for using the Design DNA prompt and token kit.
questions: What final typeface choice should replace the tested-but-unconfirmed pairing?
What exact generated-render standard is expected for high-warmth backgrounds?
What are the referenced section 6 quality gates?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents machine-loadable deploy-kit files, how to use them, governing constraints, and unresolved confirmations.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make a broader design voice/style system operational for generation workflows while preventing off-DNA visual drift.
novelty: It frames brand/design guidance as a deployable prompt-and-token kit with dials, invariants, and negative/fail prompt blocks.
contribution: Contributes the orientation layer for the Design DNA kit: the file manifest, generation sequence, governing signal-economy rule, invariant-checking expectation, and pending confirmation items.
summary_for_embedding: This README documents a Design DNA deploy kit of tokens and prompts, with usage steps, invariant checks, a signal-economy rule, and open design confirmations.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to help a content-generation system load and apply the Design DNA files consistently across modalities.
